item.title: Bring back the calls for deficit reduction. Even if they’re just words.
item.description: The political stage has a spot for someone who can seize this issue.

item.title: Fresh discoveries about parenthood
item.description: Edith Pritchett cartoon on raising an infant.

item.title: Ukraine could be a big win for Trump
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin is weakened. Now is the moment to push him to end the war.

item.title: How weddings got so expensive
item.description: The average wedding now costs $36,000, and many couples feel like they are being taken for a ride. From weekend bottlenecks and customization to family dynamics and the pressure to get everything right, the answer is more complicated than simple price gouging. Host Megan McArdle explores why modern weddings have become so expensive.

item.title: Unqualified
item.description: Michael Ramirez cartoon on Bill Pulte, mortgage fraud.

item.title: The energy permitting bill that won unanimous support in Congress
item.description: If only lawmakers applied the same zeal to reduce barriers for other energy projects.

item.title: The murder of Henry Nowak shows the danger of ‘equity’ in policing
item.description: Smearing people who expect equality before the law as “far right” or “racist” is wrong.

item.title: The new spy chief has the one qualification Trump cares about
item.description: Bill Pulte has no intelligence experience. But he has proved he’ll target the president’s foes.

item.title: Will the World Cup be a disaster? I sense a familiar panic.
item.description: Once the ball is kicked, the chaos and controversy outside the stadium tends to disappear.

item.title: Former U.S. Army secretary: What America cannot win wars without
item.description: As new tech evolves, the Army still needs full support.

item.title: Trump says his uncle was a ‘super genius.’ D-Day proves his point.
item.description: John Trump, an engineer and MIT professor, changed the course of history.

item.title: Ebola is spreading in ways no one fully grasps. Use what I know.
item.description: As cases rapidly grow, a massive deployment of resources is critical.

item.title: Don’t tell Trump, but the U.S. is still a WHO member
item.description: The World Health Organization has deftly handled attempts by countries to leave.

item.title: Alarming cruelty reported at Delaney Hall demands accountability
item.description: The federal government has an obligation to ensure immigrant detainees are not subjected to inhumane conditions.

item.title: Did Trump win the late-night comedy battle?
item.description: Did President Donald Trump kill late-night television shows, or did comedy podcasts simply beat them at their own game? In this episode of "Make It Make Sense," James Hohmann, Carine Hajjar and Damir Marusic explore the massive cultural shift in comedy during the Trump era.

item.title: Mexico’s Claudia Sheinbaum has found her scapegoat
item.description: Anti-U.S. rhetoric helps her avoid accountability ahead of elections.

item.title: ‘Mine’s self-driving’
item.description: Mark Wood cartoon on self-driving cars.

item.title: What Tuesday told MAGA and the Democratic establishment
item.description: Trump lost one, Democrats dodged some: Here's what the primary results revealed.
